items.title: The Korean Telecom Giant at the Center of Anthropic’s Mythos Controversy
items.summary: Days before Anthropic took its most advanced AI models offline, the White House ordered the company to revoke SK Telecom’s access to Claude Mythos over claims of alleged ties to China.

items.title: Operating a Humanoid With Your Body Is a Hot Job in China’s Hardware Capital
items.summary: In Shenzhen, workers at IO-AI Tech control humanoid robots using a VR rig reminiscent of Ready Player One.

items.title: No, I Don’t Want to Watch Your Straight Hockey Show
items.summary: From Amazon’s Off Campus to Netflix’s upcoming Icebreakers, the recent spate of hetero hockey romances shows Hollywood learned the wrong lessons from Heated Rivalry.

items.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming — now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI
items.summary: Chi-Hua Chien has spent more than two decades as a venture capitalist, but he thinks like a cultural anthropologist.

items.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
items.summary: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

items.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
items.summary: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

items.title: Iran sends tankers loaded with oil past US military blockade
items.summary: Three Iranian tankers loaded with crude oil have passed the US blockade line in the Gulf of Oman, ship-tracking data shows.

items.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
items.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

items.title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
items.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

items.title: Jeffrey Gundlach says Fed's Warsh is not going to be the 'easy money' chairman many hoped for
items.summary: Gundlach said Warsh's stance reduces the risk of overly accommodative monetary policy that could reignite inflation and push longer-term borrowing costs higher.

items.title: Chairman Warsh abstains from giving rate forecast as several members signal a hike in 2026
items.summary: The median projection called for the federal funds rate to end 2026 at 3.8%, a quarter percentage point above the current target range.

items.title: Chairman Warsh drastically alters Fed rate statement. Here's what's changed
items.summary: This is a comparison of Wednesday's Federal Open Market Committee statement with the one issued after the Fed's previous policymaking meeting in April.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: The Memo: Hawks lament ‘disaster’ on Iran as Trump strains to sell deal
items.summary: President Trump tried to make the case for his interim peace deal with Iran on Wednesday, but a lengthy news conference only revealed that no last-minute rabbits would be pulled from the hat to assuage his critics. Trump’s comments, delivered as he prepared to leave a Group of Seven (G7) summit in France, also exposed…

items.title: Trump signs MOU with Iran ending war while at Versailles with Macron
items.summary: President Trump on Wednesday signed the memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the U.S. and Iran ending the war between the two countries while visiting French President Emmanuel Macron at Versailles. The details of the MOU were shared earlier Wednesday by senior administration officials in a call to reporters. Trump personally signed the copy of the…

items.title: NATO chief downplays US military cutbacks
items.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday the U.S. cutting the number of troops and equipment it would provide to traditional allies in the event of an attack will not have an immediate impact. “This is not about where forces and assets are currently located,” Rutte told reporters in Brussels, according to The Associated Press. “It’s…

items.title: Chevron Takes 70% Interest in Greek Offshore Exploration Block
items.summary: Chevron Takes 70% Interest in Greek Offshore Exploration Block

items.title: Superconductivity breakthrough could unlock ultra-efficient electronics
items.summary: A clever nanoscale redesign may have solved one of superconductivity’s biggest problems. Researchers in Sweden discovered that by subtly sculpting the surface beneath an ultrathin superconducting material, they could make it stay superconducting at higher temperatures and under much stronger magnetic fields.

items.title: Humans may have hidden regenerative powers
items.summary: Scientists have taken a surprising step toward unlocking regeneration in mammals, showing that the ability to rebuild complex body parts may not be lost after all—it may simply be switched off. Using a two-stage treatment, researchers redirected the body’s normal healing response away from scar formation and toward regrowth, successfully restoring bone, joints, ligaments, and tendons after amputation in animal studies.

items.title: Scientists discover spider that disguises itself as a parasitic fungus
items.summary: Scientists have discovered a new Amazonian spider with an astonishing disguise: it looks like a parasitic fungus. The species, Taczanowskia waska, mimics both the appearance and behavior of the fungus, helping it stay hidden from predators and potentially catch prey more easily.

items.title: A deadly fungus that can infect cats and people is spreading
items.summary: It’s just a matter of time before Sporothrix brasiliensis reaches the U.S. a CDC expert says.

items.title: A ‘Super El Niño’ may be on the way. What does that mean?
items.summary: Past super El Niños have brought bad flooding, deadly fires and disease outbreaks. Climate experts already expect “shockingly high” temps this winter.

items.title: The oldest known plague outbreak struck hunter-gatherers 5,500 years ago
items.summary: Plague DNA in ancient graves near Siberia's Lake Baikal suggests the disease threatened people long before farming and crowded settlements.
